questions.stem: The asymptotic Bode magnitude plot for a minimum phase system G(s) = K(s+a)/[s(s+b)] is shown in Fig. Q1 (initial slope -20 dB/dec, magnitude 40 dB at ω=0.1, pole at ω=2, zero at ω=10, final slope -20 dB/dec). Draw the pole-zero plot of this system.
common_errors: Misidentifying the pole and zero frequencies from slope changes
Forgetting the origin pole (integrator) from the initial -20 dB/dec slope
questions.solution_method: Read break frequencies from slope changes: slope starts at -20 dB/dec (pole at origin), changes to -40 dB/dec at ω=2 (pole at s=-2), then recovers to -20 dB/dec at ω=10 (zero at s=-10). Plot pole at origin, pole at -2, zero at -10 on s-plane.
key_formulas: Slope change of -20 dB/dec → pole; +20 dB/dec → zero

questions.stem: For the system G(s) = K(s+a)/[s(s+b)] with Bode plot as in Fig. Q1, find the value of K.
common_errors: Using wrong reference frequency for the 0 dB intercept calculation
Confusing K (overall gain) with K' (Bode form gain)
questions.solution_method: Express G(s) in Bode standard form G(s) = K'(1+s/10)/[s(1+s/2)]. Use the given magnitude at ω=0.1: 20log(K') - 20log(0.1) = 40 dB → K'=10. Convert to G(s)=10(1+s/10)/[s(1+s/2)] = 10(s+10)/[s·10·(s+2)/2] = (s+10)/[s(s+2)]·... → K=2.

questions.stem: If the gain crossover frequency of G(s) = K(s+10)/[s(s+2)] (unity feedback) is 4.5 rad/s, find the phase margin in degrees.
common_errors: Sign error in phase angle: forgetting -90° from the integrator pole
Using wrong formula: PM = 180° + ∠GH, forgetting the +180°
Evaluating tan⁻¹ in wrong order (zero vs pole contribution)

notes: Single question with 3 sub-parts (A/B/C), 6 marks total, 12-minute quiz. Marker note: alternative approach for part B finding K=2.02 (using |G(jω_gc)|=1 condition) should also receive full marks.
